title: Why languages disappear
explanation:
Why languages disappear

Linguists estimate that nearly half of the world's languages could disappear within a century. Many small communities switch to dominant languages for education, work and digital communication. When fewer children learn a language at home, its vocabulary, stories and cultural knowledge may be lost forever. Preservation projects record oral histories, publish dictionaries and support bilingual schools. Although these efforts cannot save every language, they can maintain cultural diversity and respect for minority communities.

questions.text: Nearly half of the world's languages could vanish within [*a century|a week|a day].
Communities may switch to dominant languages for education and [*work|sport only|cooking only].
Loss of language can mean loss of stories and [*cultural knowledge|building materials|car engines].
Preservation projects include recording oral histories and publishing [*dictionaries|novels only|menus only].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: When fewer children learn a language at home, it may ____.  | *disappear | expand | translate
Digital communication is one factor pushing communities to switch ____.  | *languages | jobs | cities
Bilingual schools are one form of ____.  | *support | punishment | competition
The writer views preservation efforts as [*valuable but limited|useless|harmful].
questions.feedback: Cannot save every language but still valuable.

questions.text: The passage is mainly about [*language loss|airport design|computer games].
Cultural [*diversity|pollution|inflation] is one benefit of preservation.
